name: R & A Hitchcock Busing
districts_or_customers: likely Wyalusing Area SD or Lackawanna Trail SD (NE PA / Wyoming-Bradford county area)
recent_news: FMCSA USDOT 560840 record flipped to Inactive as of Nov 10, 2025; DBA 'R&A Hitchcock Busing LLC'; only 2 buses / 2 drivers / ~35k miles in 2024 - micro operator winding down.
fit_notes: Sub-scale 2-bus mom-and-pop owner-operator in Laceyville, PA; carrier marked Inactive at FMCSA in Nov 2025 suggesting succession/wind-down. Too small to be a Summit tuck-in on its own, but could be route-acquisition opportunity if Summit has nearby footprint.

name: Mauthe Busing Inc
districts_or_customers: likely Clarion-Limestone Area SD or Clarion Area SD (Clarion County, western PA)
recent_news: Family-run operation in Strattanville (Clarion County); founder Bill Mauthe died ~25 years before 2022; daughter Barbara George and her husband James now operate the buses, with granddaughter Margaret Brocious also driving full-time and running adjacent Mauthe's Country Corner store (opened 2016).
fit_notes: Multi-generational family micro-operator (~17 employees) in rural Clarion County, PA. Owners are second-generation with third-gen also active; succession path partially internal. Small for a standalone tuck-in but could bolt into a Summit Western PA cluster; family also runs a retail business which is a non-core distraction signal.

name: S V L Bussing Inc
districts_or_customers: likely Blue Ridge SD (Great Bend / Hallstead / New Milford, Susquehanna County, NE PA)
recent_news: No public web presence found; no website, no news coverage, no FMCSA hits under this exact name. Address (99 Maple St, Great Bend, PA) sits within Blue Ridge SD service area.
fit_notes: Sub-scale (~17 employees) NE PA contractor; presumed independent family operator but unverified. No digital footprint - typical of an aging mom-and-pop in northeast PA. Possible succession candidate but would require direct outreach to confirm ownership, fleet, and contract status with Blue Ridge SD.

name: Tri-County Bus Line
districts_or_customers: potentially East Stroudsburg Area SD, Stroudsburg Area SD, or Pleasant Valley SD (Monroe County, Pocono region)
recent_news: No web presence found at the Stroudsburg listing. Not to be confused with Tri County Transportation of Northern Cambria PA (acquired by Beacon Mobility in 2022) - that is a different company in western PA. The Monroe County entity has no website, news, or FMCSA listing locatable.
fit_notes: Likely small independent Pocono-region contractor (Monroe County, PA), but cannot confirm operational status or ownership. Address uses an old 'R D 7' rural route format on Route 611, which can suggest a long-tenured/legacy operator. Worth a direct call to verify but probably sub-scale on its own.

name: Lakeland Coach Lines Inc
principals: historical: Vern Behselich (co-founder partner with Wendell & Dorothy Nelson, sold his share in 1961)
districts_or_customers: Palmyra-Eagle Area School District (Jefferson/Walworth County, WI)
recent_news: Multi-generation Behselich-family school bus and coach operation in Palmyra, WI; Behselich family roots in school transportation in the area trace back to the 1940s-1961 era as Nelson's Bus Service partner. D&B lists 1975 founding (likely re-incorporation under current name) with ~$856k revenue and ~25 employees.
website: https://www.lakelandbus.com/ (NOTE: this URL belongs to a different Dover, NJ operator; Lakeland Coach Lines of Palmyra WI has no confirmed live website)
fit_notes: 50-year-old independent Behselich family operator with single-district concentration on Palmyra-Eagle Area SD plus some charter work; ~25 employees / sub-$1M revenue. Outside Summit's core PA/NE footprint but in WI expansion territory. Classic succession/tuck-in profile (multi-generation owner-operator, small geographic concentration). Note: USDOT 2015314 appears to cover only the company's farm-supply/private property side; the school-bus operation runs under separate authority - confirm fleet size in diligence.
